sources.notes: Construct a low-cost sequence of operations.
Official tutorial constructs operations by choosing a base and grouping residues.
Maspy discusses trying bases and finding a good balance around base 63..120.

annotation.summary: Output a sequence of operations with total cost below the required bound.
annotation.constraints: The output budget is tight enough that naive binary-base constructions are too expensive.
annotation.core_idea: Use a base-b construction. In each round, group numbers by residues modulo b^t and apply short operations that force them to the next multiple of b^t. Increasing b reduces the average length of each operation but changes the fixed penalties; trying a range of b values finds one below the cost limit.
annotation.complexity: O(n log_b n) generated operations plus brute-force search over a small base range.
tricks: trade operation length against fixed penalty

solution_variants.name: base-search-construction
solution_variants.summary: Try several bases, generate residue-group operations, and output the first construction within budget.

tags.evidence: The solution is an explicit operation schedule construction.
The base parameter is found by trying a moderate finite range.
Operations are organized by residues and multiples of powers of the chosen base.
